For count_factors: use a generator expression — sum(1 for i in range(1, n + 1) if n % i == 0). This counts every i that divides n with no remainder.

count_factors must be a top-level function (not nested inside another function or class). ProcessPoolExecutor pickles the function to send it to workers, and lambdas or nested functions can't be pickled.
For factor_counts: open a ProcessPoolExecutor with a 'with' block. Call pool.map(count_factors, numbers) to run count_factors on each number and collect results in order.
pool.map returns results in the same order as the input. Use zip(numbers, pool.map(...)) and pass it to dict() to pair each number with its count.
The 'if __name__ == "__main__":' guard at the bottom prevents a recursive spawn loop on Windows/macOS. Always include it when using ProcessPoolExecutor.
